channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to bring Apple’s ‘Hide My Email’ privacy to Android and Windows
item.description: Have you ever wanted to sign up for an online service but you didn’t want to provide your real email address as part of the process? There’s a good chance your email address has your actual name in it. Or perhaps you want to avoid the risk of getting spammed.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: A private European aerospace startup completes the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle
item.description: A rocket by a private European aerospace company launched from Norway on Sunday and crashed into the sea 30 seconds later.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After Pure Storage, CIA-backed VC invests in ceramic-based startup that wants to build Exabyte-class storage
item.description: CIA-backed In-Q-Tel invests in ceramic-based storage startup Cerabyte to advance ultra-durable archival solutions for government files.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Solar Power Cybersecurity Threats Exposed: Millions of Inverters Vulnerable to Attacks
item.description: Millions of solar power systems face security risks due to vulnerable inverters, cloud services, and monitoring platforms. Attackers can manipulate energy production, disrupt grids, and steal sensitive data.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New 3D technology could soon bring surgeons closer to patients in Africa's most remote regions
item.description: A groundbreaking initiative could soon be bringing surgeons closer to patients in Africa's remote corners where the doctor-to-patients ratio is among the lowest in the world.
item.dc:creator: Naa Adorkor Cudjoe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This may be the most private and secure cloud-based AI solution around
item.description: What if you could build a business-specific generative AI (genAI) solution that was hosted in the cloud? What if that cloud used the most energy-efficient systems in the world? And what if those services were also all running on the world’s most secure computing platform, the Mac? Wonder no more, b
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Convicted of bilking investors, Nikola founder and Trump donor gets a presidential pardon
item.description: The founder of an electric car start-up sentenced to prison for exaggerating the potential of his technology has been pardoned by President Donald Trump, potentially wiping out hundreds of millions of dollars in restitution that prosecutors were seeking for bamboozled investors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Windows 11 roadmap will tell you exactly when to expect Microsoft’s next annoying feature
item.description: Microsoft wants to clarify exactly what’s happening in terms of new features coming to its desktop OS with a new Windows Roadmap portal.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Layoffs erase more than 600 Bay Area tech, biotech, hotel, retail jobs
item.description: A fresh wave of layoffs jolted the Bay Area during March with several rounds of cutbacks that erased well over 600 jobs.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Tesla Protests Planned Globally Amid Rise of Hate - A Worldwide Response
item.description: Protests against President Trump, Elon Musk, and the rise of hate are planned globally for Friday and Saturday, with a focus on dismantling parts of the federal government and promoting peaceful resistance.
item.dc:creator: Rick Hurd, The Mercury News

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These 5 new AI tools can help you do everything from managing tasks to improving your public speaking
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Hundreds of AI tools emerge every week. I’ve picked five new ones worth exploring.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: La transformación digital, esencial para la supervivencia del sector asegurador
item.description: Los beneficios de la transformación digital ya son palpables para el sector asegurador. Así lo constata el informe Perspectivas de tecnología en seguros 2025: transformando la innovación en ventaja competitiva que firma Concentrix.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: La Diputación de Girona optimiza el uso de sus recursos mediante la automatización
item.description: El Departamento de Intervención de la Diputación de Girona ha implementado de la mano de Atos un proyecto de automatización de procesos (RPA).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: China's Tron 1 robot hurdles over obstacles like they're nothing
item.description: Tron 1, a Chinese company's two-legged robot, is versatile and can walk, roll and pivot, even on rough terrain. Tron 1 stands 33 inches tall and weighs 44 pounds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: G2 Names INE 2025 Cybersecurity Training Leader
item.description: INE, a global leader in networking and cybersecurity training and certifications, is proud to announce it is the recipient of twelve badges in G2’s Spring 2025 Report, including Grid Leader for Cybersecurity Professional Development, Online Course Providers, and Technical Skills Development, which h
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nvidia-backed CoreWeave slashes IPO size and price amid investor concerns
item.description: CoreWeave plans to reduce the size of its U.S. initial public offering and price its shares below the indicated range, a person familiar with the matter told Reuters on Thursday, dampening expectations that the listing would boost investor appetite for IPOs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk privately pressured Reddit CEO Steve Huffman on content moderation: report
item.description: X owner Elon Musk was privately messaging with Reddit CEO Steve Huffman while also putting public pressure on the social media company’s content moderation efforts, The Verge reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The slim phone era is (finally) back
item.description: Smartphones have been around long enough that, to the casual observer, their designs seem to have hit a plateau. And on a functional level, that’s more or less true—we’re all essentially holding the same six-inch-ish rectangle, aside from the occasional foldable exception.
item.dc:creator: Sam Byford, Fast Company

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The G7's Declining Tech Competitiveness: A Warning Sign for the Future
item.description:
The G7 nations are falling behind in key tech economic indicators, endangering their future competitiveness, according to a new study by SThree and Cebr.")

Decline of the G7's Tech Supremacy

The "Group of 7" (G7) nations are no longer at the forefront of technological innovation and economic progress. A recent study by London-based workforce consultancy SThree and the Center for Economics and Business Research (Cebr) has revealed that these developed economies are falling behind in key tech indicators, including high-tech exports, software developers, and AI-related patent filings.

Not a single G7 nation made it into the top 10 in any of these categories, which is a clear warning sign for their future competitiveness. The study warns that without a focus on innovation and future industries, tech leadership by the G7 is no longer guaranteed.

The Rise of Emerging Tech Hubs

Tech advances are reshaping the global economy, with industries such as AI, automation, and clean energy relying heavily on a STEM-skilled workforce. As a result, countries investing in science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) education and training will drive growth; those that don't may fall behind.

Emerging tech hubs like Singapore, Ireland, and Australia are leading the way in fostering tech innovation, surpassing all G7 nations. These countries have invested heavily in STEM education, research partnerships, and industry-friendly policies, which has enabled them to attract top talent and drive economic growth.

Singapore's Success Story

One country that stands out as a model for tech innovation is Singapore. With its highly developed infrastructure, business-friendly environment, and strong education system, Singapore has become a hub for startups and established companies alike.

The government has actively promoted STEM education, research partnerships, and industry-friendly policies, which has enabled the country to attract top talent and drive economic growth. As a result, Singapore has produced a number of successful tech unicorns, including Lazada, Grab, and Ninja Van.

The Importance of Investing in STEM

STEM skills are essential for driving innovation and economic progress. By investing in education and training programs, governments can help build a skilled workforce that can drive growth and improve living standards.

Success depends on collaboration between governments, businesses, and education to build a skilled STEM workforce. This can be achieved through partnerships, research collaborations, and industry-friendly policies that promote innovation and economic progress.

The Future of Work: Skills-Based Hiring

A growing number of organizations are adopting skills-based hiring practices, which prioritize skills gained through alternative methods over traditional college degrees. This approach helps companies find and attract a broader pool of candidates better suited to fill positions long term.

Skills-based hiring also opens up opportunities for non-traditional candidates, including women and minorities. By dropping traditional degree requirements, companies can tap into a more diverse talent pool and drive greater innovation and economic progress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Qualcomm launches global antitrust offensive against Arm, accusing it of stifling competition
item.description: Qualcomm has launched a global antitrust offensive against Arm Holdings, accusing its longtime partner of anti-competitive practices in regulatory complaints filed across three continents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: FBI warns about document conversion sites spreading malware
item.description: The FBI warns that some free online document converters load malware onto people's computers, making their private information vulnerable to attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Databricks’ TAO method to allow LLM training with unlabeled data
item.description: Data lakehouse provider Databricks has unveiled a new large language model (LLM) training method, TAO that will allow enterprises to train models without labeling data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft’s newest AI agents can detail how they reason
item.description: If you’re wondering how AI agents work, Microsoft’s new Copilot AI agents provide real-time answers on how data is being analyzed and sourced to reach results.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 big takeaways from Nvidia GTC
item.description: Now that the dust has settled from Nvidia’s GTC 2025, a few industry experts weighed in on some core big picture developments from the conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Managing and monitoring user accounts on Linux
item.description: There are a number of commands on Linux that you can use to manage user accounts and monitor user activity. This post provides details on the commands that you need to know if you are managing a Linux server and the user accounts on that system.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: World Backup Day is March 31
item.description: Are you ready? World Backup Day is March 31, a day that has been recognized every year since it was first set up in 2011.
item.dc:creator: Network World
